title: Kelo v. City of New London, 545 U.S. 469 (2005) — opinion (Cornell Legal Information Institute)
author: Supreme Court of the United States (opinion of the Court by Stevens, J.)
organization: Cornell Law School — Legal Information Institute
bias_or_interest: Primary judicial record; Cornell LII is a neutral redistributor.
quotation_note: Public-domain U.S. government work; short attributed quotes are safe.
rights_note: Opinion text is public domain. Cite Cornell LII as the access source.
notes: Primary opinion (No. 04-108). Confirms 5-4, Jun 23 2005, Stevens; Kennedy concurrence; O'Connor + Thomas dissents; economic development = 'public use'; Fort Trumbull, not blighted. Capture durable hash before publish.

title: Kelo v. City of New London — Oyez case record
organization: Oyez (IIT Chicago-Kent College of Law / Cornell LII / Justia)
bias_or_interest: Educational legal database; secondary summary of the primary record.
quotation_note: Summarize; do not copy long passages.
notes: Cross-check for vote, date, facts (NLDC, Pfizer, redevelopment plan).

title: Kelo v. City of New London — case page (Institute for Justice)
organization: Institute for Justice (IJ) — public-interest law firm; the Kelo plaintiffs' counsel
bias_or_interest: ADVOCACY: IJ represented the homeowners and campaigns on eminent domain. Use for attributed case-background and aftermath facts (house relocation, reform counts), not as neutral fact on policy.
quotation_note: Attribute explicitly; do not state advocacy framing as undisputed fact.
notes: Plaintiffs' counsel; advocacy source. House relocation (2008) and 'little pink house' framing attributed here.

title: Enacted Legislation Since Kelo — state eminent-domain reforms (Institute for Justice)
bias_or_interest: ADVOCACY tally of post-Kelo state reforms. Use for the attributed, ranged state-reform count; note many reforms were criticized as weak.
relevant_locations: Post-Kelo state legislation / constitutional amendments tally
quotation_note: Attribute; present as a range (~40-plus states), not a single hard number.
notes: State-reform count varies by source/time (IJ cites up to ~47 states / ~11-12 constitutional amendments). Use a range and attribute.
